title: Subjunctive in relative clauses
objectives: Use subjunctive when the antecedent is unknown or nonexistent
Use indicative when the antecedent is specific and known
grammar_notes: INDICATIVE when the antecedent EXISTS and is specific: 'Tengo un amigo que habla cinco idiomas.' (I have a specific friend.)
SUBJUNCTIVE when the antecedent is hypothetical, sought, or doesn't exist: 'Busco un amigo que hable cinco idiomas.' (I'm looking for one — may not exist.)
'No hay nadie / nada que ...' always takes subjunctive (the antecedent doesn't exist).
example_phrases: Necesito un piso que tenga balcón.
Conozco a una persona que vive en Tokio. (indicative — known)
No hay nadie que sepa la respuesta. (subjunctive — none exists)
